body:
ACKNOWLEDGMENT + STANDING ORDER (S4 completion).

ACKNOWLEDGED, all of it: the 3B.5 revert was the correct call -- restoring the phase was structurally right, but it exposed that the phase was never production-viable (117k-combo grid x 36 contexts, synchronous, no time budget). The revert to known-safe no-op with a redesign flag is exactly the right triage. Formally logged as open design work: Phase 3B.5 redesign (time-budgeted, spread across timer events, or materially smaller grid) before any re-enable. Also acknowledged: SLS(22) SEG+ERM delivery per directive, and both SLS(23) hardening layers now complete across all 8 sections -- 29 further real defects closed, including the short-side probability blindness, the save/load 8-vs-9 misalignment, the PRI stale-numbers arm, and the pattern-recognition discard. Superb, honest work throughout, including stopping the CLI guessing when demo orders started firing.

STANDING ORDER -- execute autonomously, no further confirmation needed:
1. The market is closed (weekend). At the next market open (Sunday ~21:00 UTC), the SLS(23) .ex4 (18:06Z build, the one with both review layers complete) must be on the USDJPY M5 chart. If Manuel has not manually swapped it by then, send one relay reminder flagging exactly what to drag where; do not attempt further headless-attach experiments -- that avenue is closed per your own finding.
2. Once SLS(23) is attached and the market is open: run S4 to completion -- state invalidation, full fresh calibration Phases 1-6, ghost validation to verdict. This is the first-ever full run of the corrected system; expect materially different behavior from all prior calibrations (three phases awake minus 3B.5, loss-learning active, midrank ICs, pooled ProbDB, short-side buckets now real).
3. Report the consolidated S4 result in one relay message: calibration duration (quantify the midrank speedup vs historical runs), IC deltas where visible, ghost WR/PF/trade count/gate verdict, derived growth rate with inputs including the ERM cost haircut, SEG feed status, ERM warmup state, and any anomaly.
4. Demo only throughout. Live attach remains gated on Manuel's explicit go-ahead after the S4 numbers are reviewed.

If S4 passes its gate, the deliverable after your report is nothing -- hold and await review. If S4 fails its gate, diagnose, report, and hold; no autonomous re-arm loops.
